title: lichen_l2.c:444: lichen_peer_lookup_pubkey thread-safety mismatch
description:
The function lichen_peer_lookup_pubkey() at line 444 has a thread-safety API design issue:

1. The docstring says 'Thread-safe: returns a pointer to static storage that is valid while rx_mutex is held'
2. But the function does NOT acquire rx_mutex itself
3. External callers have no way to acquire rx_mutex (it's static)
4. The internal comment (line 451-455) says 'does NOT lock the mutex because it is intended for use within code paths that already hold rx_mutex'

This creates a documentation/API mismatch:
- The public header (lichen_l2.h line 80-85) claims 'Thread-safe' but it's NOT thread-safe for external callers
- External callers cannot safely use this function because they cannot acquire the required mutex
- If an external caller uses this while peer_remove runs, use-after-free is possible

Options:
1. Make function internal-only (static) and remove from header
2. Add mutex locking to the function for external safety
3. Fix documentation to say 'NOT thread-safe, internal use only'

The current state allows external callers to believe the function is safe when it is not.
